question: Will Ethereum reach $7,500 by December 31, 2026?
description:
This market will immediately resolve to “Yes” if any Binance 1-minute candle for Ethereum (ETH/USDT) between November 24, 2025, 14:00 and December 31, 2026, 23:59 in the ET timezone has a final “High” price equal to or greater than the price specified in the title. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No.”

The resolution source for this market is Binance, specifically the ETH/USDT “High” prices available at:
https://www.binance.com/en/trade/ETH_USDT with the chart set to “1m” (one-minute candles) on the top bar.

Please note that the outcome of this market depends solely on the price data from the Binance ETH/USDT trading pair. Prices from other exchanges, different trading pairs, or spot markets will not be considered for the resolution of this market.

question: Labour leadership election scheduled by March 31?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if a leadership election for the UK-wide Leader of the Labour Party is scheduled by the specified date, ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

This market is about whether a date for any Labour Party leadership election is announced within this market's timeframe. Whether the election is supposed to take place within this market's timeframe or later will have no effect on the resolution to this market.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from the Labour Party, however a consensus of credible reporting may be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: No Labour Party leadership election is currently scheduled, with traders assigning low implied probabilities to near-term contests reflecting Keir Starmer's strengthened position after the July 2024 general election landslide. Recent developments bolstering sentiment include stable polling leads for Labour despite controversies over winter fuel cuts and donor issues, alongside no formal leadership challenges from within the parliamentary party or unions. Starmer's recent shadow cabinet reshuffle and policy pivots on welfare signal internal cohesion. Key upcoming events include the Labour conference (September 21-24), autumn budget (October 30), and local elections in May 2025, which could test unity but are unlikely to trigger a vote absent a major scandal or no-confidence motion.

question: Blackburn Beavers vs. Eastern Illinois Panthers
description:
In the upcoming CBB game, scheduled for December 1 at 8:00 PM ET:

If the Blackburn Beavers win, the market will resolve to "Blackburn Beavers".

If the Eastern Illinois Panthers win, the market will resolve to "Eastern Illinois Panthers".

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed.

If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50-50.

The result will be determined based on the final score including any overtime periods.

events.description:

eventMetadata.context_description: Blackburn Beavers hold a slim 55% implied probability edge as traders weigh their superior recent form against Eastern Illinois Panthers in this college football matchup. Eastern Illinois, meanwhile, snaps a two-game skid marred by offensive line injuries to key starters, per official reports, hampering their run game against Blackburn's aggressive front seven. Head-to-head history favors Blackburn 2-1, with home-field advantage and rest edge further tilting trader consensus toward the Beavers despite the Panthers' FCS pedigree.

Realistic upsets remain negligible: only an undeniable, universally witnessed supernatural event could shift odds, but with under two years left, traders see no catalysts on the horizon.

question: Will the Republican Party win the CA-22 House seat?
description:
This market will resolve according to the party of the candidate who wins the CA-22 congressional district seat in the U.S. House of Representatives in the 2026 midterm elections. The midterm elections will take place on November 4, 2026.

​​A candidate's party will be determined by their ballot-listed or otherwise identifiable affiliation with that party at the time all of the 2026 House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources. A candidate without a ballot-listed affiliation to either the Democrat or Republican parties will be considered a member of one of these parties based on the party with which they most recently expressed their intent to caucus at the time all of the House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources.

This market will resolve based on the result of the election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the United States government, specifically the Federal Election Commission (https://www.fec.gov/).

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket gives Democrats a 73% implied probability of winning California's 22nd Congressional District House seat, reflecting recent polling momentum for challenger Rudy Salas over incumbent Republican David Valadao. Latest surveys, including an Emerson College poll from late October showing Salas ahead 47%-43% among likely voters, have shifted odds toward Democrats in this Central Valley battleground, where Valadao prevailed by under 2% in 2022. Key drivers include Salas's fundraising edge—over $3 million raised versus Valadao's $1.5 million—and strong Latino voter outreach amid high early turnout. National House generic ballot trends favoring Democrats further bolster sentiment, though historical volatility and undecideds leave room for shifts before November 5.

This market will resolve according to the winner of the Democratic primary election for the mayor of Washington D.C., scheduled to be held June 16, 2026.

If no 2026 D.C. Democratic Mayoral Primary takes place, this market will resolve to “Other.”

The resolution source for this market will be the first official announcement of the results from the D.C. Democratic Party; however, an overwhelming consensus of credible reporting may suffice.

events.title: Who will win the 2026 Democratic D.C. Mayoral Primary?
events.description:

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket positions D.C. Councilmember Kenyan McDuffie as the frontrunner at 50% implied probability to win the 2026 Democratic D.C. Mayoral Primary, with Ward 4's Janeese Lewis George trailing closely at 39.5%, driven by early polling leads and fundraising momentum amid incumbent Mayor Muriel Bowser's diminished prospects from ongoing federal probes into city contracts and spending. McDuffie's centrist appeal and recent campaign launch event have boosted his edge over George's progressive base in Ward 4, while Council Chair Phil Mendelson holds 10.8% as a potential establishment pick if he enters. Bowser's 1% odds reflect trader skepticism on her reelection bid, with no formal announcement yet; upcoming filings and debates could shift dynamics.

question: Will Antonio Reynoso be the Democratic nominee for NY-07?
description:
This market will resolve according to the candidate who wins the nomination for the Democratic Party to contest the NY-07 congressional district seat in the U.S. House of Representatives in the 2026 midterm elections. The Democratic primary will take place on June 23, 2026.

If no nominee is announced by November 3, 2026, 11:59PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of official Democrat sources, including https://democrats.org/.

Any replacement of the nominee before election day will not change the resolution of the market.

events.title: NY-07 Democratic Primary Winner

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus heavily favors Claire Valdez at 66% implied probability to win the NY-07 Democratic primary, driven by her commanding lead in recent internal polls, robust grassroots organizing in Brooklyn and Queens neighborhoods like Williamsburg and Bushwick, and a fundraising edge exceeding $1.5 million. Antonio Reynoso trails at 24%, supported by his Brooklyn Borough President role and institutional endorsements from labor groups and local Democratic clubs. Recent catalysts include Valdez's viral debate performance boosting her momentum and Reynoso's union-backed ad campaign, while lower-tier candidates like Tiffany Cabán and Julie Won gain from progressive activist networks. Odds reflect uncertainty ahead of the June 25 contest, with turnout key in this left-leaning district.

question: Will the People Power Party (PPP) win the 2026 South Korean local elections?
description:
South Korean local elections are scheduled to be held on June 3, 2026. 

This market will resolve according to the party whose official candidates win the most head of local government (Mayor or Governor) elections for South Korea’s upper-level local governments during these elections.

A candidate will be considered an official candidate of a party if they are officially nominated by that party and are registered for the relevant election in affiliation with that party. Independent candidates will not count for any party.

South Korea’s upper-level local governments include the following cities and provinces:

Cities (mayoral elections): Seoul, Busan, Daegu, Incheon, Gwangju, Daejeon, Ulsan, & Sejong

Provinces (governor elections): Gyeonggi, North Chungcheong, South Chungcheong,  North Jeolla, South Jeolla, North Gyeongsang, South Gyeongsang, Jeju, & Gangwon.

A party will have won as soon as it becomes mathematically impossible for any other party to equal or surpass its number of wins in these elections.

In the case of a tie between two or more parties for the greatest number of head of local government elections won, this market will resolve in favor of the party whose English name comes first in alphabetical order, as listed in this market group.

Resolution of this market will be based on the results of the relevant elections, once those results are official. This market will remain open until a party has won or until the results of all of the relevant elections are made official. If the results of any of the relevant 2026 South Korean local elections aren’t known by January 31, 2027 11:59 PM ET, the winning party will be determined based on the available results up to that point. If none of the results of the relevant 2026 Korean local elections are known by that time, this market will resolve to “Other”.

This market will resolve based on the results of the elections as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the South Korean government, specifically the National Election Commission.
